notes: All 11 reported dropped edges traced back to LLM-emitted dangling references in raw batches. None required new node creation — the targets already existed in the graph with mismatched ID prefixes (e.g., the LLM emitted `file:...README.md` as `document:...README.md` because the file is a Markdown doc, but then referenced it from other edges as `file:`).

9 of the 13 raw dangling edges were fixed by re-targeting to existing nodes with the correct prefix (`file:` -> `document:...README.md`; `config:` -> `file:...pom.xml`; `service:` -> `file:...docker-compose.yml`). These edges were net new — they did not exist in the graph before review.

4 of the 13 raw dangling edges (the Java test files) had *both* wrong source AND wrong target paths: the LLM emitted `src/main/java/...Test.java` when the actual test files live under `src/test/java/`. The correct test files and classes do exist in the graph (with proper `src/test/java` paths). Re-targeting to the correct test paths added 4 net-new edges: 2 `contains` (file -> class) and 2 `tested_by` (test file -> production file).

Cross-batch `imports` edge coverage is 100%: all 188 edges from the import map are present in the graph, and both endpoints of every import-map edge are in-scope (474 files analyzed). The graph has 197 `imports` edges total — 9 extra are LLM-detected barrel/index re-exports not captured by the static import map (e.g., `i18n/index.ts` re-exporting from sub-modules).
Final graph: 840 nodes, 1074 edges, 0 dangling edges, no unknown node types, complexity values all normalized to {simple, moderate, complex}.

No nodes were added or recovered during review — all targets already existed in the graph. The 11 reported issues were purely edge-side: edges referencing nodes that exist under a different ID prefix or with a corrected file path.
